commit.message:
fix: strings.Builder, error wrapping audit, EnsureUser idempotency

TASK-16: Replace string concatenation with strings.Builder in
BuildCacheFlags for fewer allocations.

TASK-18: Audit all fmt.Errorf calls — getKopiaRepositoryByName was
already fixed during TASK-01 refactoring. Fixed remaining %v in
test/utils/utils.go. All internal code now uses %w consistently.

TASK-20: Fix EnsureUser to propagate createUserOnServer errors instead
of silently swallowing them. Previously, non-ServerNotReady errors were
logged but the secret name was returned as success, masking the failure.
Now the error is returned so the reconciler properly requeues. The
create-user shell script is already idempotent (checks if user exists
before creating), verified by a new test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: CI pipeline split, plugin overhaul, CRD validation, manageCniPlugin (#33)

* ci: strip image push from CI, add plugin matrix to release

CI workflow changes:
- Remove push-to-main trigger (CI now runs on PRs and workflow_dispatch only)
- Remove GHCR login step and image push conditionals from build job
  (set push: false always -- CI builds only warm the GHA cache)
- Remove the manifest job (no images to create manifests for)
- Remove image_tags from version job outputs (no longer consumed)
- Drop packages: write permission (only contents: read needed)

Release workflow changes:
- Add a plugin matrix job that builds kubectl-unbounded-net for all
  6 OS/arch combinations in parallel with proper Go build/mod caching,
  replacing the sequential inline build loop in the release job
- Release job now depends on [version, build, manifest, plugin] and
  downloads plugin artifacts instead of building them inline
- Go caching already present on the release job's render-manifests step

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: simplify ZSH completion to not override kubectl

The previous approach hijacked kubectl's compdef, which broke kubectl's
own tab completion and caused OS names (darwin/linux/windows) to appear
as completions. kubectl automatically dispatches completion to plugin
binaries via __complete, so we only need completion for the standalone
kubectl-unbounded-net binary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: overhaul kubectl plugin completion, help, and create commands

Completion:
- Add kubectl_complete-unbounded-net symlink for native kubectl plugin
  tab completion protocol
- Remove completion subcommand (kubectl handles it via the symlink)
- Disable Cobra's default completion command
- Hide inherited kubeconfig flags from completion output
- Add ValidArgsFunction with <name> placeholder for all create commands
- Add active help hints for CIDR, duration, and selector flag values
- Add selectable completions for --type, --dry-run, --output,
  --tunnel-protocol

Help and usage:
- Fix duplicate error output (SilenceErrors)
- Show 'kubectl create <resource>' in usage when invoked via symlink
- Show 'kubectl unbounded net' (no hyphens) to match kubectl display
- Add Long descriptions and Examples to all five create commands
- Indent Long text two spaces in help output
- Add aliases (st, gp, spr, sgpa, gpp) to create subcommands
- Expand flag descriptions with format hints and examples

Symlink management:
- Add kubectl_complete-unbounded-net to symlink create list
- Auto-replace dangling symlinks without --force
- Add -f shorthand for --force

Create command changes:
- Rename --manage-cni-plugin to --no-manage-cni-plugin (default is true)
- Add --pod-cidr-block as required flag
- Add example for sites with existing CNI plugin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: require podCidrAssignments in Site CRD, document manageCniPlugin

CRD validation:
- podCidrAssignments is now required (minItems=1) instead of optional
- cidrBlocks within podCidrAssignments is required (removed omitempty)
- Document that pod CIDR assignment is disabled when manageCniPlugin
  is false, regardless of assignmentEnabled on individual rules

Documentation:
- Update manageCniPlugin behavior table to include Pod CIDR Assignment
- Add explanation of manageCniPlugin interaction with assignmentEnabled
- Add YAML example for sites with an existing CNI plugin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: skip pod CIDR assignment when manageCniPlugin is false

The controller now skips pod CIDR allocation for nodes in sites with
manageCniPlugin set to false. The podCidrAssignments entries are still
required to define the CIDR ranges for inter-site routing, but the
controller will not assign per-node pod CIDRs or modify node specs.

This ensures that sites using an external CNI plugin (e.g. Cilium,
Calico) do not have their pod CIDRs overwritten by the controller.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

feat: add GitHub Pages deployment — play in the browser

Web deployment using Pyodide (Python WASM) + xterm.js terminal:
- docs/index.html: landing page with xterm.js terminal

synchronous input(). Colorama is mocked with raw ANSI codes that

feat: operator best practices — PVC watcher, phase-based reconciler, webhooks

TASK-06: Break up monolithic Reconcile into phase methods
- Extract handleFinalizer, validateDependencies, reconcileServerCredentials,
  reconcileBackupResources as separate phases with clear inputs/outputs
- Remove PVC auto-creation fallthrough from backup reconciler

TASK-08: Smarter requeue strategy with repository watches
- Stop requeuing for permanent validation errors (PVC/repo not found)
- Add KopiaRepository watch with field indexer so backups re-reconcile
  when their referenced repository appears or changes
- Add findBackupsForRepository and findBackupsForPVC map functions

TASK-10: Add validating webhooks for KopiaBackup and KopiaRepository
- KopiaBackup: validate cron schedule (5 fields) and PVC name (DNS subdomain)
- KopiaRepository: validate storage-type-specific required fields
- Webhook server behind ENABLE_WEBHOOKS env var (default: enabled)
- Add config/webhook and config/certmanager scaffolding

TASK-11: Separate PVC watcher into dedicated controller
- New internal/controller/pvcwatcher package with its own reconciler
- Watches PVCs for label/annotation changes, auto-creates/deletes KopiaBackups
- Handles schedule annotation sync and duplicate prevention
- 10 test cases covering all paths

TASK-19: Record events for finalizer cleanup failures
- Emit warning event when server user deletion fails during finalization

commit.message:
fix: ramp arrow icon not matching instruction modifier (#406)

* fix: ramp arrow icon not matching instruction modifier (#255)

The leaflet-routing-machine helper _leftOrRight, used when converting
on/off ramp steps, was purely binary: anything that didn't contain
'left' was returned as 'Right'. This meant an 'on ramp straight'
maneuver ended up with modifier 'Right', rendering a right-turn arrow
even though the instruction text said 'straight'.

Monkey-patch _leftOrRight on the router instance so that modifiers
containing neither 'left' nor 'right' (e.g. 'straight') are passed
through unchanged. 'straight' then becomes the 'Straight' modifier and
getIconName correctly maps it to the continue (straight) arrow icon.

Also add a dedicated test suite covering all modifier cases including
the straight regression.

gps: 14 Hz output rate, 1 Hz PPS timepulse

GPS output rate 16->14 Hz (GPS_NOMINAL_MS 63->71 ms): at 16 Hz the
chip was missing ~20-24 samples per 256-sample buffer (~14.6 Hz
actual with multi-constellation). 14 Hz sits safely below the
chip's reliable output rate, eliminating fill samples.

PPS timepulse 25->1 Hz (period 1_000_000 us): the timepulse is only
used for RTC clock sync via GPIO interrupt, so 1 Hz is sufficient
and reduces unnecessary ISR load.

commit.message:
refactor: replace prediction engine with calibrated GBM model

BREAKING: Prediction engine completely overhauled to fix absurd scenarios
(e.g. NVDA predicted at $1000/share for bull scenario).

Root causes fixed:
- HMM with only 252 days of data produced unreliable regime estimates
- No bounds on p5/p95 percentiles → extreme values passed to UI
- √T extrapolation (scalePercentiles) amplified errors exponentially
- Raw 1-year historical mean used as future expected return

New architecture:
- Primary model: Calibrated GBM with Student-t quantiles (ν=5)
  - Closed-form percentiles, no Monte Carlo needed
  - Drift shrinkage: blend historical μ with 8% equity prior (w=dataYears/10)
  - Damped volatility for horizons >2yr (mean-reversion à la Fama & French)
  - Hard sanity bounds: [-80%,+100%]/year, [-95%,+1000%] total
- Secondary: Block bootstrap for short horizons (≤6 months)
- HMM demoted to informational regime display only
- GARCH retired from ensemble (no value beyond ~20 trading days)

Expected results (NVDA ~$140, σ≈50%, 12mo):
- Before: Bear -70%, Bull +150% (or worse)
- After: Bear ~-38%, Bull ~+58%

commit.message:
fix: clock-set spin loop — store now not egps.time in state

set_from_egps stored egps.time (GPS PVT timestamp) in LocationState
after a successful RTC set. If EGPS_TIME was stale (GPS time >10s in
the past), (now - egps.time) > LOCATION_DIFF was true again on the
very next call, causing an infinite re-trigger loop.

Fix: store 'now' (wall-clock at time of set) so the 10s cooldown is
measured from when the set actually happened. Also rate-limit the
NaiveDateTime::from_timestamp_millis failure path.

commit.message:
feat: Build improvements (#142)

Addresses two recent feedback items:
- zig pollutes the user's .cache/zig directory, which may grow large
- Selene manifests take too long to write

The first item is managed by providing zig with a cache dir, which
subsequently sets `ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR` in the subprocess environment.

The second is managed by providing a `complete_manifest` bool flag to
`build()`. When True, the manifest aims to be a complete picture of the
build stages, for use with sharing and diagnosis. When False (default),
less information is written to the manifest. At current this reduced
information is in the form of stripping input artifact or intermediate
artifacts in the manifest, keeping only the final binary.

* feat: Implement TC Status message and J1939-81 compliance fixes

would not be discovered by implements when starting after the Virtual Terminal.

Call set_single_gnss_gps() during GPS init (before set_output_rate) to
disable all non-GPS constellations (GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou, SBAS,
QZSS). With multiple constellations the MAX-M10S caps at ~10 Hz nav
solutions, causing ~30% of 25 Hz epochs to be missed.

Patch Cargo.toml to use local max-m10s-rs for the new method.

commit.message:
Add set_single_gnss_gps() for 25 Hz reliable output

Enable GPS-only mode (disable GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou, SBAS, QZSS)
via CFG-SIGNAL VALSET messages. With multiple constellations the
MAX-M10S can only sustain ~10 Hz nav solutions; single-GNSS mode
allows reliable 25 Hz output.

Split into two VALSET calls (3 signals each) to stay within the
Apollo3 I2C FIFO 32-byte limit.
